title: cerastium semidecandrum var. arenarium
intro: Cerastium semidecandrum var. arenarium, also known as sand-knawel or dune mouse-ear, is a perennial herb belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to sandy areas in Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Cerastium semidecandrum var. arenarium include its ability to treat urinary disorders, such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate conditions like arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create remedies for a range of health issues.
